key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion enables robust weld penetration state prediction across fundamentally different welding processes, including TIG and laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need to relabel new process data and supports transferable intelligent monitoring for multi-process welding systems.
method: Train a UDA model to align source and target feature distributions without target labels.
Use gradual source domain expansion to improve adaptation stability across process shifts.
Evaluate same-process and cross-process penetration classification on dedicated TIG and laser datasets.
evidence_signal: The method achieved 90.65% and 90.72% average accuracy in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG→Laser plus 81.13% Laser→TIG in cross-process transfer, outperforming the supervised baseline by about 35–43% absolute.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of the source and target welding datasets, and adaptation may still be sensitive when process physics differ strongly or data are scarce.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
